reasoning: The resolution requires South Sudan to ratify the AfCFTA by July 1, 2025. It is May 2, 2025, leaving less than two months. Multiple sources from late 2024 and early 2025 (e.g., Africa Spectator in Jan 2025) list South Sudan among the few remaining countries that have not ratified. There is no public record of the cabinet or parliament currently processing the ratification. Given the timeline, standard legislative sluggishness, and South Sudan's current massive domestic crises (including economic strain, suspended oil exports, and neighboring warfare spillover), pushing through a complex trade agreement ratification in the next 60 days is highly implausible. Thus, the probability is set to the absolute floor.
